page: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. The server is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The CPU supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks advanced vector and matrix extensions such as AVX or AMX. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, but it is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included.

Performance benchmarks place the D4ps_v5 in the average to weak tiers depending on the workload. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance scored 1821.99 and multi-core scored 7240.75, both landing in the average category. Memory bandwidth tests showed average performance for large-block reads at 50.19 GB/sec and writes at 29.49 GB/sec, but weak performance for small-block cached reads at 283.1 GB/sec. Geekbench 5 scores were weak, with a single-core score of 1110.0 and a multi-core score of 3595.0. Cryptographic performance was poor, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU encryption score of 651.26. LLM inference speeds for a small 135M model were also low, yielding 162.09 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 49.35 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a balanced resource density of 4 vCPUs to 16 GB of RAM, offering a utility-focused profile for workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets or heavy vector mathematics. The instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as basic database operations, where it achieved an average PassMark database score of 2726.47, as well as lightweight web serving and single-threaded compression tasks. However, it is less suited for intensive cryptographic applications, software compilation, or machine learning inference due to its lower benchmark scores in those areas.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage, and contains no GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average results in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside weak performance in Geekbench, software compilation, and cryptographic tests. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for standard utility workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, and basic database operations that do not require specialized x86 instruction sets or high-performance vector extensions.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, designed for general-purpose workloads and basic database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, suitable for general-purpose and basic database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual machine offering balanced compute and memory resources for standard utility workloads and lightweight database operations.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated ARM64 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage integration
Delivers average multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, and basic database tasks
